channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 10 years in, the Try Guys expand their universe with a new streaming platform
item.description: Ten years ago, four Buzzfeed staffers took the site’s pivot to video as an opportunity to film themselves trying things they’d never done before. With a video of them trying on women’s underwear that went viral, the Try Guys were born.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Christensen, Fast Company

item.title: India gearing up to be AI powerhouse as data center giant pledges $3.2 billion investment to add 550MW of capacity by 2030
item.description: Major $3.2 billion investment will expand India's data centre capacity in India by 550MW, responding to the rising demand for digital infrastructure and cloud services.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is the stock market in an AI bubble?
item.description: When it comes to AI investments, “a lot of money is going to get wasted,” MIT professor Daron Acemoglu told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Why the OpenAI-to-Anthropic pipeline remains so strong
item.description: The pipeline from OpenAI to Anthropic remains strong as ever, with cofounder Durk Kingma becoming the latest high-profile figure to make the move. On Tuesday, Kingma, one of the lesser-known cofounders of OpenAI, joined Anthropic, best known for developing the rival AI chatbot Claude.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Bay Area labor organizer uses tech to help governments better support people in poverty
item.description: "If you can get a Peloton interest-free, you should be able to pay government debt interest-free," says Promise cofounder and CEO Phaedra Ellis-Lamkins.
item.dc:creator: Kate Bradshaw, The Mercury News

item.title: De la ‘sala de máquinas’ a la junta directiva: el nuevo rol del CIO, a debate en el CIO Summit 2024
item.description: Los debates marcaron, una vez más, gran parte de la agenda de la última edición de CIO Summit 2024, celebrado en Madrid los pasados 25 y 26 de septiembre.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why airlines are turning to Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi
item.description: Last week, Air France was the latest major airline to announce it will be moving its in-flight Wi-Fi to the Starlink network, offering it for free to members of its frequent flier program.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kuhr, Fast Company

item.title: Things get nasty in lawsuit between WordPress.org and WP Engine
item.description: After years of nasty back and forth accusations between WordPress hosting provider WP Engine (WPE) and WordPress.org, the open source project that provides the software, on Wednesday WPE sued WordPress.org in federal court, accusing WordPress.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.94 improves file search
item.description: The September 2024 release of Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, features improvements for finding files using the File Explorer. The upgrade also introduces the ability to run Python tests with coverage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why Uber and Lyft drivers are using risky DIY Tesla robotaxis
item.description: A self-driving Tesla carrying a passenger for Uber rammed into an SUV at an intersection in suburban Las Vegas in April, an accident that sparked new concerns that a growing stable of self-styled “robotaxis” is exploiting a regulatory gray area in U.S. cities, putting lives at risk.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: OMB gives agencies new considerations when buying AI
item.description: The Office of Management and Budget told agencies in a new memo to proactively manage AI risks, promote a competitive market and manage business processes. The post OMB gives agencies new considerations when buying AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence
item.description: How is the Army leveraging OSINT to strengthen its intelligence capabilities? The post OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: What’s new for Apple Intelligence?
item.description: Most Apple watchers may have noticed that the company’s iPhone 16 marketing really does put Apple Intelligence front and center, even though its home-baked breed of AaI (Artificial [Apple] Intelligence) isn’t available quite yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming
item.description: DoJ says its policy will encourage independent security and safety research, but will large AI companies follow suit in encouraging vulnerability disclosure? The post DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI raises $6.6 billion in fresh funding as it moves away from its nonprofit roots
item.description: OpenAI says it has raised $6.6 billion in venture capital investments as part of a broader shift by the ChatGPT maker away from its nonprofit roots.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fix for BGP routing insecurity ‘plagued by software vulnerabilities’ of its own, researchers find
item.description: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) is not yet the simple fix for the security weaknesses of the internet’s Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) many in the communications industry think it is, a team of German researchers has warned.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM opens first quantum computing center in Europe
item.description: IBM unveiled its first IBM Quantum Data Center outside the United States – and its second ever – in Ehningen, Germany.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What to expect from Apple this October and beyond
item.description: The Apple product machine continues to whirr, with a host of new products, including Macs, iPads, and iPhone SE, expected to appear this fall.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iPhone's satellite messaging: A lifeline in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iOS 18’s satellite messaging helps isolated residents seek help in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: The battle cry of 2025: Do cloud local!
item.description: Microclouds can have many names and offer many features. They can be hosted by a regional cloud provider or specialize in a specific use case, such as the rise of GPU cloud providers focusing on the exploding AI space.
item.dc:creator: Info World
